abstract: Human-AI ecologies involve human and AI-based agents that coordinate their interactions in part by following social norms. Social norms, therefore, are important for establishing the guardrails that ensure desirable interactions in a way that is consistent with essential values, such as human safety. Managing human-AI ecologies requires specifying norms to enable coordination in known situations but also allowing for the emergence of norms to enable coordination in unspecified, unstructured situations. We integrate predictive processing theory and social norm theory to explain how existing norms are enacted and reinforced based on agents’ predictive models and how new norms emerge as agents update their predictive models in response to prediction errors in uncertain coordination scenarios. Rooted in this perspective, we develop a design theory that emphasizes design for predictability and propose a set of design principles for managers and developers to encode norms to evolve in human-AI ecologies, monitor outcomes, and intervene when necessary.

affiliation: University of Applied Sciences St. Gallen
authors_full.name: Thomas Grisold

ob.ai_reason: Human-AI生态规范与协调设计，组织行为学与AI交互核心议题
ob.tldr: 人-AI生态系统中，人类与AI智能体通过社会规范协调互动，规范是确保符合安全等核心价值的"护栏"。研究整合预测加工理论与规范理论，解释既有规范如何基于智能体的预测模型被实施与强化，以及新规范如何在不确定性协调场景中涌现。基于此提出"设计以增强可预测性"的理论框架，为管理者和开发者提供编码演化规范、监测结果及必要干预的设计原则。

title: Guardrails for Human-AI Ecologies: Norm-Based Coordination and Design for Predictability
title_zh: 人机AI生态的护栏：基于规范的协调与可预测性设计
